title: Central Time Zone

infobox.value: is a [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") in parts of [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"), [Central America](/wiki/Central_America "Central America"), and a few [Caribbean islands](/wiki/Caribbean_Islands "Caribbean Islands").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is one hour behind the [Eastern Time Zone](/wiki/Eastern_Time_Zone "Eastern Time Zone").

is observed from the first Sunday in November to the second Sunday in March. It is [six hours](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9206:00 "UTC−06:00") behind [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time") (UTC) and designated internationally as UTC−6.[[2]](#cite_note-2) From the second Sunday in March to the first Sunday in November, the same areas observe [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") (DST), creating the designation of **Central Daylight Time (CDT)**, which is [five hours](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9205:00 "UTC−05:00") behind UTC and known internationally as UTC−5.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Time zone in North America

This article is about the time zone in North America. For other time zones, see [Central Time](/wiki/Central_Time_(disambiguation) "Central Time (disambiguation)").

| Central Time Zone | |
| --- | --- |
| [Time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") | |
| Central Standard Time Note that most of Mexico abolished DST | |
| UTC offset | |
| CST | [UTC−06:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9206:00 "UTC−06:00") |
| CDT | [UTC−05:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9205:00 "UTC−05:00") |
| Current time | |
| 12:56, 26 March 2026 CST [[refresh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Central_Time_Zone&action=purge)] 13:56, 26 March 2026 CDT [[refresh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Central_Time_Zone&action=purge)] | |
| Observance of [DST](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") | |
| DST is observed in some of this time zone. | |

The **Central Time Zone** is a [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") in parts of [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"), [Central America](/wiki/Central_America "Central America"), and a few [Caribbean islands](/wiki/Caribbean_Islands "Caribbean Islands").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is one hour behind the [Eastern Time Zone](/wiki/Eastern_Time_Zone "Eastern Time Zone").

Daylight saving time is observed in most parts of that zone (20 states in the US, three provinces or territories in Canada, and several border municipalities in Mexico): **Central Standard Time (CST)** is observed from the first Sunday in November to the second Sunday in March. It is [six hours](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9206:00 "UTC−06:00") behind [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time") (UTC) and designated internationally as UTC−6.[[2]](#cite_note-2) From the second Sunday in March to the first Sunday in November, the same areas observe [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") (DST), creating the designation of **Central Daylight Time (CDT)**, which is [five hours](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9205:00 "UTC−05:00") behind UTC and known internationally as UTC−5.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Regions using Central Time (CT)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Regions using Central Time (CT)")]

### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Canada")]

Main article: [Time in Canada](/wiki/Time_in_Canada "Time in Canada")

The province of [Manitoba](/wiki/Manitoba "Manitoba") is the only [province or territory in Canada](/wiki/Provinces_and_territories_of_Canada "Provinces and territories of Canada") that observes Central Time in all areas.

The following Canadian provinces and territories observe Central Time in the areas noted, while their other areas observe [Eastern Time](/wiki/Eastern_Time_Zone "Eastern Time Zone"):

* [Nunavut](/wiki/Nunavut "Nunavut") (territory): western areas (most of [Kivalliq Region](/wiki/Kivalliq_Region "Kivalliq Region") and part of [Qikiqtaaluk Region](/wiki/Qikiqtaaluk_Region "Qikiqtaaluk Region"))
* [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario") (province): a portion of the northwest bordering southeastern Manitoba, in and around [Kenora](/wiki/Kenora "Kenora").

Also, most of the province of [Saskatchewan](/wiki/Saskatchewan "Saskatchewan") is on Central Standard Time year-round, never adjusting for Daylight Saving Time. One major exception includes [Lloydminster](/wiki/Lloydminster "Lloydminster"), a city whose borders overlap both [Alberta](/wiki/Alberta "Alberta") and Saskatchewan. The city charter[[4]](#cite_note-4) stipulates that it shall observe [Mountain Time](/wiki/Mountain_Time_Zone "Mountain Time Zone") and DST, putting the community on the same time as all of Alberta (UTC−7) in the winter, and in time with Saskatchewan (UTC−6) during the summer.

### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: United States")]

Main article: [Time in the United States](/wiki/Time_in_the_United_States "Time in the United States")

Ten states are contained entirely in the Central Time Zone:

* [Alabama](/wiki/Alabama "Alabama")
* [Arkansas](/wiki/Arkansas "Arkansas")
* [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois")
* [Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa")
* [Louisiana](/wiki/Louisiana "Louisiana")
* [Minnesota](/wiki/Minnesota "Minnesota")
* [Mississippi](/wiki/Mississippi "Mississippi")
* [Missouri](/wiki/Missouri "Missouri")
* [Oklahoma](/wiki/Oklahoma "Oklahoma")
* [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin")

Five states are split between the Central Time Zone and the [Mountain Time Zone](/wiki/Mountain_Time_Zone "Mountain Time Zone"):

* [Kansas](/wiki/Kansas "Kansas"): all except for [Sherman](/wiki/Sherman_County,_Kansas "Sherman County, Kansas"), [Wallace](/wiki/Wallace_County,_Kansas "Wallace County, Kansas"), [Greeley](/wiki/Greeley_County,_Kansas "Greeley County, Kansas"), and [Hamilton](/wiki/Hamilton_County,_Kansas "Hamilton County, Kansas") counties
* [Nebraska](/wiki/Nebraska "Nebraska"): eastern two thirds including the east part of [Cherry County](/wiki/Cherry_County,_Nebraska "Cherry County, Nebraska")
* [North Dakota](/wiki/North_Dakota "North Dakota"): all except for southwest regions, south part of [McKenzie County](/wiki/McKenzie_County,_North_Dakota "McKenzie County, North Dakota"), and the majority of [Dunn County](/wiki/Dunn_County,_North_Dakota "Dunn County, North Dakota") and far western [Sioux County](/wiki/Sioux_County,_North_Dakota "Sioux County, North Dakota")
* [South Dakota](/wiki/South_Dakota "South Dakota"): eastern half including part of [Stanley County](/wiki/Stanley_County,_South_Dakota "Stanley County, South Dakota")
* [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas"): all except for [El Paso](/wiki/El_Paso_County,_Texas "El Paso County, Texas"), [Hudspeth](/wiki/Hudspeth_County,_Texas "Hudspeth County, Texas"), and northwestern [Culberson](/wiki/Culberson_County,_Texas "Culberson County, Texas") counties

Five states are split between the Central Time Zone and the [Eastern Time Zone](/wiki/Eastern_Time_Zone "Eastern Time Zone"):

* [Florida](/wiki/Florida "Florida"): [Florida panhandle](/wiki/Florida_panhandle "Florida panhandle") west of the [Apalachicola River](/wiki/Apalachicola_River "Apalachicola River"); [Franklin County](/wiki/Franklin_County,_Florida "Franklin County, Florida") west of the Apalachicola River and the northern parts of [Gulf County](/wiki/Gulf_County,_Florida "Gulf County, Florida") are in the Central Time Zone
* [Indiana](/wiki/Indiana "Indiana"): [Northwest Indiana](/wiki/Northwest_Indiana "Northwest Indiana") and most of [Southwestern Indiana](/wiki/Southwestern_Indiana "Southwestern Indiana"), including the [Evansville metropolitan area](/wiki/Evansville_metropolitan_area "Evansville metropolitan area")
* [Kentucky](/wiki/Kentucky "Kentucky"): the western portion of the state, including three major cities: [Bowling Green](/wiki/Bowling_Green,_Kentucky "Bowling Green, Kentucky"), [Owensboro](/wiki/Owensboro,_Kentucky "Owensboro, Kentucky") and [Paducah](/wiki/Paducah,_Kentucky "Paducah, Kentucky")
* [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"): The four [Upper Peninsula](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula_of_Michigan "Upper Peninsula of Michigan") counties bordering Wisconsin: [Gogebic](/wiki/Gogebic_County,_Michigan "Gogebic County, Michigan"), [Iron](/wiki/Iron_County,_Michigan "Iron County, Michigan"), [Dickinson](/wiki/Dickinson_County,_Michigan "Dickinson County, Michigan"), and [Menominee](/wiki/Menominee_County,_Michigan "Menominee County, Michigan") counties
* [Tennessee](/wiki/Tennessee "Tennessee"): [West Tennessee](/wiki/West_Tennessee "West Tennessee") and [Middle Tennessee](/wiki/Middle_Tennessee "Middle Tennessee"), including the major cities of [Nashville](/wiki/Nashville,_Tennessee "Nashville, Tennessee"), [Memphis](/wiki/Memphis,_Tennessee "Memphis, Tennessee"), and [Clarksville](/wiki/Clarksville,_Tennessee "Clarksville, Tennessee")

Additionally, [Phenix City, Alabama](/wiki/Phenix_City,_Alabama "Phenix City, Alabama"), and several nearby communities in [Russell County, Alabama](/wiki/Russell_County,_Alabama "Russell County, Alabama"), unofficially observe Eastern Time. This is due to their close proximity to [Columbus, Georgia](/wiki/Columbus,_Georgia "Columbus, Georgia"), which is on Eastern Time.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Although legally located within the Central Time Zone, [Kenton, Oklahoma](/wiki/Kenton,_Oklahoma "Kenton, Oklahoma")—located to the adjacent east of the defined border of the Central and Mountain time zones (at the Oklahoma−[New Mexico](/wiki/New_Mexico "New Mexico") state line)—unofficially observes Mountain Time.[[6]](#cite_note-EOHC-Cimmaron-6) This is reportedly because most people who interact with the town reside in either New Mexico or [Colorado](/wiki/Colorado "Colorado").[[6]](#cite_note-EOHC-Cimmaron-6)[[7]](#cite_note-Last-7)

### Mexico

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Mexico")]

Main article: [Time in Mexico](/wiki/Time_in_Mexico "Time in Mexico")

Most of Mexico—roughly the eastern three-fourths—lies in the Central Time Zone, except for [five northwestern states](/wiki/States_of_Mexico "States of Mexico") ([Baja California](/wiki/Baja_California "Baja California"), [Baja California Sur](/wiki/Baja_California_Sur "Baja California Sur"), [Sinaloa](/wiki/Sinaloa "Sinaloa"), [Sonora](/wiki/Sonora "Sonora"), and most of [Nayarit](/wiki/Nayarit "Nayarit")) and one southeastern state ([Quintana Roo](/wiki/Quintana_Roo "Quintana Roo")). It is known locally as the *Zona Centro*.[[8]](#cite_note-law2022-8)

The federal entities of [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") that observe Central Time:

* [Aguascalientes](/wiki/Aguascalientes "Aguascalientes")
* [Campeche](/wiki/Campeche "Campeche")
* [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_(state) "Chihuahua (state)")[[9]](#cite_note-9) – Excluding the municipalities of [Janos](/wiki/Janos_Municipality "Janos Municipality"), [Ascensión](/wiki/Ascensi%C3%B3n_Municipality "Ascensión Municipality"), [Juárez](/wiki/Ju%C3%A1rez_Municipality,_Chihuahua "Juárez Municipality, Chihuahua"), [Guadalupe](/wiki/Guadalupe_Municipality,_Chihuahua "Guadalupe Municipality, Chihuahua"), and [Práxedis Gilberto Guerrero](/wiki/Pr%C3%A1xedis_G._Guerrero_Municipality "Práxedis G. Guerrero Municipality"), which observe [Mountain Time](/wiki/Mountain_Time_Zone "Mountain Time Zone") with [DST](/wiki/Daylight_Savings_Time "Daylight Savings Time"). Additionally, counties east of Guadalupe observe Daylight Savings in Central Time.
* [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila")
* [Colima](/wiki/Colima "Colima") – except for the [Revillagigedo Islands](/wiki/Revillagigedo_Islands "Revillagigedo Islands"), in which the inner islands use UTC−7 and the outer island uses UTC−8
* [Chiapas](/wiki/Chiapas "Chiapas")
* [Durango](/wiki/Durango "Durango")
* [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato")
* [Guerrero](/wiki/Guerrero "Guerrero")
* [Hidalgo](/wiki/Hidalgo_(state) "Hidalgo (state)")
* [Jalisco](/wiki/Jalisco "Jalisco")
* [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City")
* [Michoacán](/wiki/Michoac%C3%A1n "Michoacán")
* [Morelos](/wiki/Morelos "Morelos")
* [Nayarit](/wiki/Nayarit "Nayarit") – \*only the municipality of [Bahía de Banderas](/wiki/Bah%C3%ADa_de_Banderas "Bahía de Banderas"), rest of the state uses UTC−7
* [Nuevo León](/wiki/Nuevo_Le%C3%B3n "Nuevo León")
* [Oaxaca](/wiki/Oaxaca "Oaxaca")
* [Puebla](/wiki/Puebla "Puebla")
* [Querétaro](/wiki/Quer%C3%A9taro "Querétaro")
* [San Luis Potosí](/wiki/San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD "San Luis Potosí")
* [State of Mexico](/wiki/State_of_Mexico "State of Mexico")
* [Tabasco](/wiki/Tabasco "Tabasco")
* [Tamaulipas](/wiki/Tamaulipas "Tamaulipas")
* [Tlaxcala](/wiki/Tlaxcala "Tlaxcala")
* [Veracruz](/wiki/Veracruz "Veracruz")
* [Yucatán](/wiki/Yucat%C3%A1n_(state) "Yucatán (state)")
* [Zacatecas](/wiki/Zacatecas "Zacatecas")

### Central America

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Central America")]

Out of the seven countries comprising [Central America](/wiki/Central_America "Central America"), all but one use Central Standard Time year-round:

* [Belize](/wiki/Belize "Belize"), [Costa Rica](/wiki/Costa_Rica "Costa Rica"), [El Salvador](/wiki/El_Salvador "El Salvador"), [Guatemala](/wiki/Guatemala "Guatemala"), [Honduras](/wiki/Honduras "Honduras"), and [Nicaragua](/wiki/Nicaragua "Nicaragua").[[10]](#cite_note-factbook2023-10)

[Panama](/wiki/Panama "Panama"), which recognizes Eastern Standard Time (UTC−5) year round, is the exception.[[10]](#cite_note-factbook2023-10)

## Central Daylight Time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Central Daylight Time")]

"Central Daylight Time" redirects here. For other uses, see [Central Daylight Time (disambiguation)](/wiki/Central_Daylight_Time_(disambiguation) "Central Daylight Time (disambiguation)").

[Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") (DST) is in effect in much of the Central time zone between mid-March and early November. The modified time is called "Central Daylight Time" (CDT) and is [UTC−05:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9205:00 "UTC−05:00").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In the United States, all time zones that observe DST were effectively changed by the [Energy Policy Act of 2005](/wiki/Energy_Policy_Act_of_2005 "Energy Policy Act of 2005"). Beginning in 2007, DST would now begin at 2 a.m. (02:00) on the second Sunday in March instead of the first Sunday in April, moving the time from 2 to 3 a.m. (i.e., from 02:00 to 03:00). Additionally, DST would end at 2 a.m. (02:00) on the first Sunday in November instead of the last Sunday in October, moving the time from 2 to 1 a.m. (i.e., from 02:00 to 01:00).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

At the time, Mexico decided not to go along with [this change](/wiki/Energy_Policy_Act_of_2005 "Energy Policy Act of 2005") and observed their *[horario de verano](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time_in_Mexico "Daylight saving time in Mexico")* ("summer schedule") from the first Sunday in April to the last Sunday in October. In December 2009, the Mexican Congress allowed ten border cities, eight of which are in states that observe Central Time, to adopt the US daylight time schedule effective in 2010. In October 2022, however, CDT was used in Mexico for the last time after DST was abolished. The US is also seeing traction in the same direction, with the [Sunshine Protection Act](/wiki/Sunshine_Protection_Act "Sunshine Protection Act") that proposes legislation that would permanently end the switching of times and make daylight saving time permanent.[[11]](#cite_note-11)
